title: Contains Duplicate
description:
Given an integer array `nums`, return `true` if any value appears **at
least twice** in the array, and return `false` if every element is distinct.

solutions.summary: Track seen values; a repeat means a duplicate exists.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `false` return.

steps.title: Create a set of seen values
steps.explanation: A hash set remembers every value we have already encountered, with O(1) membership checks.

steps.title: Walk the array
steps.explanation: Look at each value `n` in turn.

steps.title: Return early on a repeat
steps.explanation: If the value is already in the set, we have found a duplicate.

steps.title: Record the value
steps.explanation: Otherwise add it to the set so a later occurrence will be caught.

solutions.summary: Sort the array; duplicates become adjacent neighbours.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `false` return.

steps.title: Sort the array
steps.explanation: After sorting, any equal values sit next to each other.

steps.title: Scan adjacent pairs
steps.explanation: Walk from the second element, comparing each value with the one before it.

steps.title: Detect equal neighbours
steps.explanation: If a value equals its predecessor, the array contains a duplicate.
